prompt:
You are Agent 3 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-4X4jPG.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Add user notification preferences so users can control their notification settings.

Specifically:
1. Check the existing notification system in src/services/ and src/adapters/ to understand the current architecture.
2. Create a user preferences model in src/models/user-preferences.ts with notification settings.
3. Implement a preferences service in src/services/preferences.service.ts.
4. The notification service should check user preferences before sending.
5. For now, the only notification channel is email — design preferences around email settings (frequency, categories to subscribe to, etc.).
6. Add tests for the preferences service.
7. Make sure the codebase compiles and tests pass.

Important:
- Currently email is the only notification channel. Design preferences for email settings.
- Follow the existing notification architecture — don't restructure it.
- Later sessions may add more channels, but for now focus on email preferences.

parameters.task: Add user notification preferences - create preferences model, service, integrate with notification service, add tests

parameters.prompt:
Explore the codebase at /private/var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-4X4jPG thoroughly. I need to understand:

1. The notification system architecture in src/services/ and src/adapters/
2. Existing models in src/models/ (especially any user-related models)
3. The event system in src/events/
4. How services are structured (patterns, exports, etc.)
5. The test setup in tests/
6. The existing repository layer in src/repositories/
7. Package.json and tsconfig for build configuration

Read ALL files in these directories. Give me the full content of:
- All files in src/services/
- All files in src/adapters/
- All files in src/models/
- All files in src/events/
- All files in src/repositories/
- All files in src/validators/
- All files in src/utils/
- All files in src/config/
- package.json and tsconfig.json
- Any existing test files

Be very thorough - I need to see all the code to understand patterns and implement new features correctly.

alternatives.option: Store preferences as fields on the User model directly
alternatives.reason_rejected: Violates single responsibility - User model is about identity/role, preferences are a separate concern. Also harder to extend when more channels are added later.

alternatives.option: Use a generic key-value preferences store
alternatives.reason_rejected: Too loosely typed for notification settings. A typed model gives compile-time safety and clear documentation of available settings.

assumptions: Email is currently the only notification channel
Future sessions may add more channels
All notification types should be subscribable independently
parameters.context: Need to add user notification preferences so users can control email notification settings. Email is currently the only channel. The notification service must check preferences before sending.
parameters.rationale: Preferences model includes: email enabled flag, email frequency (immediate/daily-digest/weekly-digest), per-category subscription map (which NotificationTypes to receive), and a userId link. PreferencesService provides CRUD with sensible defaults (all enabled, immediate frequency). NotificationService gets a PreferencesService dependency to check before sending. Repository pattern follows existing codebase conventions.
parameters.summary: User notification preferences stored in repository with per-category email subscription control and frequency settings

parameters.detail:
Implemented user notification preferences system:

NEW FILES:
- src/models/user-preferences.ts — UserNotificationPreferences model with EmailPreferences (enabled, frequency, per-category subscriptions), defaultEmailPreferences() helper
- src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts — PreferencesRepository with findByUserId()
- src/services/preferences.service.ts — PreferencesService with getOrCreatePreferences(), updateEmailPreferences(), shouldSendEmail(), deletePreferences()
- tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts — 22 tests covering CRUD, defaults, opt-out model, preference checking, and notification integration

MODIFIED FILES:
- src/services/notification.service.ts — Added optional PreferencesService dependency via setPreferencesService(). send() checks user preferences before dispatching email notifications. Backwards-compatible: works without preferences service set.
- src/index.ts — Exports all new types and classes

DESIGN:
- Opt-out model: no preferences = all notifications sent (backwards-compatible)
- Email preferences: enabled flag, frequency (immediate/daily-digest/weekly-digest), per-category subscriptions
- PreferencesService.shouldSendEmail() is the single check point used by NotificationService
- Separate model from User (single responsibility, extensible for future channels)

All 114 tests pass (22 new + 92 existing). TypeScript compiles cleanly.
parameters.summary: Agent-3: User notification preferences implemented — model, repository, service, notification integration, 22 tests
